title: Dog Constant Head Shaking and Whining: Causes and Triage Guide
meta_desc: Find out why your dog is shaking their head and whining. Learn about ear infections, hematomas, and when you need to see a vet immediately.
content_html:
What is causing my dog to shake their head and whine?

Constant head shaking and whining are clear indicators that your dog is experiencing significant discomfort or pain, most commonly localized to the ear canal or the head. This behavior is often triggered by an ear infection (otitis externa), a foreign object like a grass seed stuck in the canal, or ear mites. The whining signifies that the irritation has moved beyond a simple itch and is now causing acute distress that your pet cannot manage on their own.

Is this a veterinary emergency?

The urgency level for this condition is Medium. While head shaking is rarely immediately life-threatening, it is a sign of active pain. You should aim to see a veterinarian within 24 hours. If left untreated, violent shaking can lead to an aural hematoma (a painful blood-filled swelling of the ear flap) or permanent damage to the middle ear. Seek immediate care if the shaking is accompanied by a persistent head tilt, loss of balance, or flickering eye movements.

Why a photo helps with triage

Sharing a clear photo of your dog's ear can be vital for a remote triage assessment. A high-quality image of the inner ear flap and the opening of the ear canal allows a professional to look for signs of inflammation, crusting, or discharge color (such as black, waxy, or purulent). This visual evidence helps determine if the issue is likely a surface infection, a parasitic infestation, or an emergency involving a foreign body that requires urgent sedation to remove.

faqs.question: Should I try to clean my dog's ears at home before the vet visit?
faqs.answer: No, it is best to avoid putting any liquids or cleaners into the ear until a vet has confirmed the eardrum is intact. If the eardrum is ruptured, many common cleaners can cause permanent deafness.

faqs.question: What is an aural hematoma?
faqs.answer: An aural hematoma occurs when violent head shaking causes a blood vessel inside the ear flap to burst, filling the ear leather with blood and creating a painful, pillow-like swelling.

faqs.question: Can allergies cause constant head shaking?
faqs.answer: Yes, chronic allergies are a leading cause of recurring ear infections in dogs, which leads to the inflammation and pain that causes head shaking.
